chief_complaint: A 14-year-old girl presented with meningitis complicated by bacteremia and cerebral venous sinus thrombosis.
history: Complement factor B (FB) deficiency is an extremely rare alternative pathway defect. The patient developed Neisseria meningitidis serogroup Y meningitis. Complement function was assessed by TCA and AP50 hemolytic assay, FB quantification, and functional reconstitution with purified proteins.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: TCA: normal, AP50: undetectable, plasma FB concentration: reduced, C3 and C4: normal. Reconstitution with purified FB, but not FD, restored AP activity. Patient plasma failed to support C3b-dependent hemolysis.
